name: Triple Chip Brownies
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Bittersweet chocolate, melted and slightly cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line an 8 3/4-by-13 1/2-inch glass or metal pan with a large piece of aluminum foil, smoothing it into the corners.
3. Butter the foil.
4. In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light in color.
5. Stir in the melted chocolate.
6. Add the vanilla, then add the eggs, beating until blended.
7. In another bowl, combine the flour and salt.
8. Add the walnuts and all the bits, tossing to coat them with the flour.
9. Scrape the flour, nuts, and bits into the bowl with the butter, and stir just until all the ingredients are mixed.
10. Do not over-beat.
11. Scrape the batter into the prepared pan, holding the foil so it does not move.
12. Place the pan in the middle of the oven and bake until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out almost clean, about 50 minutes.
13. Remove the pan from the oven and let it stand for an hour.
14. Cover the pan with foil and place it in the freezer for at least 1 1/2 hours or in the refrigerator overnight.
15. Turn the cooled brownies out onto a clean workspace, lift off the foil, and cut into 32 squares.
16. Store in the refrigerator or freezer in an airtight tin.
